chunk_summary: This section explores case-based approaches for stunting workup and counseling, emphasizing chronic undernutrition, its determinants, and evidence-based interventions using authoritative guidelines and references.
learning_objectives: Understand the clinical workup process for stunting in children.
Identify key determinants of chronic undernutrition and stunting.
Apply evidence-based dietary and supplementation guidelines in counseling.
Integrate case-based strategies for managing stunting and related conditions.
key_facts: Stunting affects approximately 22% of children under 5 globally (WHO, 2023).
Zinc supplementation is recommended for managing diarrhoea, a common contributor to stunting (WHO ELENA).
Dietary guidelines for Indians (2024) emphasize balanced nutrition to prevent chronic undernutrition.
Short stature in childhood may result from genetic, nutritional, or environmental factors (Stony Brook Medicine).
Coeliac disease can contribute to stunting and requires specific dietary management (ESPGHAN).
reference_quote: Zinc supplementation is a proven intervention for managing diarrhoea, which is a significant contributor to stunting in children. (WHO ELENA)
document_key_topics: Determinants of stunting and related conditions
